channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Australia’s grey-zone problem is a thinking gap, not a capability gap | ASPI Strategist
item.description: In January, Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney told the World Economic Forum something that Western democracies have long known but rarely said in public. The rules-based international order, he observed, was always a pleasant fiction. ...

item.title: 🌏 Why China Waits | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Beijing is playing a long game on Taiwan.

item.title: 🌍 The Gulf Is Still America’s to Lose | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How U.S. partnerships can survive the war in Iran.

item.title: 🌍 U.S. Just Struck Iranian Targets Around The Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: U.S. Central Command says it carried out the strikes in retaliation to Iranian attacks on its forces. The post U.S. Just Struck Iranian Targets Around The Strait Of Hormuz (Updated) appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 The protraction trap: why a regional conflict could be hard to end | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The most dangerous assumption in current contingency planning is that any war, even a regional war involving the United States, would be short. Four interacting forces would make high-intensity regional conflicts hard to terminate: regime ...

item.title: 📍 How the Trump administration can reform the Jones Act to lower US energy costs | Atlantic Council
item.description: Shippers should be allowed to use non–Jones Act compliant vessels as long as no alternative is available. The post How the Trump administration can reform the Jones Act to lower US energy costs appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ As the US Army adds drones to formations, here’s how one base trains its operators | Defense News
item.description: Fort Stewart soldiers are figuring out how to train on unmanned systems after the base stood up the Marne Unmanned Center of Excellence.

item.title: 📍 USAF Is Going To Explore What Will Finally Replace The B-52 | The War Zone
item.description: The USAF plans to keep its B-52s until they are nearly a century old, but they cannot fly forever, and costs are already skyrocketing on key upgrades. The post USAF Is Going To Explore What Will Finally Replace The B-52 appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 US and Iran exchange fire as Trump says war will ‘be over quickly’ | Defense News
item.description: U.S. forces launched retaliatory strikes against Iranian military facilities Thursday after Iran fired missiles and drones at three U.S. Navy vessels.

item.title: 🛡️ What’s in Trump’s New Counterterrorism Strategy? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The White House ignores the threat of far-right groups.

item.title: 🌍 How the Abraham Accords Fueled a New Era of Conflict | Foreign Policy
item.description: Despite promising peace, Israeli-Gulf cooperation paved the path to war with Iran.

item.title: 📍 Starmer Braces for Upsets in U.K. Local Elections | Foreign Policy
item.description: British voters are expected to abandon the Labour Party for nonmainstream alternatives.

item.title: 🏴 Putin is dragging Belarus deeper into Russia’s Ukraine invasion | Atlantic Council
item.description: Belarus may not currently be poised to join the invasion of Ukraine, but Vladimir Putin is clearly intent on dragging the country deeper into Russia’s war effort, writes Hanna Liubakova. The post Putin is dragging Belarus deeper into Russia’s Ukraine invasion appeared first on…

item.title: 📍 With launches slated to grow a hundredfold, Space Force seeks more sites, money, people, and AI | Defense One
item.description: Even today’s accelerated pace strains decades-old launch facilities.

item.title: 🌏 China’s grey-zone fleet is eroding Taiwan’s control at sea | ASPI Strategist
item.description: China’s pressure on Taiwan increasingly relies on vessels that aren’t warships. Instead, Beijing is deploying a maritime grey-zone fleet: a network of civilian and paramilitary vessels used to harass, intimidate and probe Taiwan while remaining ...

item.title: 📍 Who Is Xi’s Real No. 2? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The Chinese leader isn’t willing to give anybody else power.

item.title: 🔐 Top Pentagon tech officials optimistic Mythos-style AI tools will improve cyber defense | Breaking Defense
item.description: Mythos is just the first of a new generation of AIs that can patch vulnerable code at superhuman speed, said Pentagon CTO Emil Michael and Cyber Policy chief Katie Sutton, downplaying the uniqueness of Anthropic.

item.title: 🌏 As the Trump-Xi summit draws closer, trade uncertainty still looms large | Atlantic Council
item.description: What China and the United States manage to agree on in Beijing on key export controls or purchase agreements will significantly shape the Trump administration’s approach to rebuilding its tariff wall. The post As the Trump-Xi summit draws closer, trade uncertainty still looms…

item.title: 🕵️ The new playbook for AI leadership: The case of the United Arab Emirates | Atlantic Council
item.description: This report is the first in a series by the Atlantic Council’s GeoTech Center assessing emerging artificial intelligence (AI) powers around the world according to a novel five-pillar framework. The post The new playbook for AI leadership: The case of the United Arab Emirates…

item.title: 🏛️ How does budget reconciliation work? Here’s everything you need to know. | Breaking Defense
item.description: Valerie Insinna outlines how Congress draws up a military spending plan for the year ahead.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon will ‘never again’ rely on a single AI provider, official says | Defense One
item.description: Emil Michael, defense undersecretary for research and engineering, said new agreements with Big Tech companies are a “counterstatement” to the ongoing Anthropic-Pentagon conflict as the agency prioritizes flexible contracts.

item.title: 🏴 Saab CEO optimistic Ukraine Gripen deal could be finalized this year | Breaking Defense
item.description: A top Ukrainian official said Kyiv was “ready” for the big fighter jet deal, but Sweden’s defense minister suggested challenges remain.

item.title: 📍 ASELSAN at SAHA 2026: Introducing next-generation multi-domain defense systems | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] Unveiled at SAHA 2026, ASELSAN’s latest portfolio reflects a layered and integrated defense architecture, bringing together electronic warfare, counter-UAV, airborne and naval capabilities within a unified operational framework.

item.title: 📍 What the EU-Mercosur trade deal’s provisional implementation means and what comes next | Atlantic Council
item.description: The agreement bridges two highly complementary but historically protectionist markets, offering benefits for both regions. The post What the EU-Mercosur trade deal’s provisional implementation means and what comes next appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ The next Army chief won’t inherit a force, he’ll inherit an argument | Breaking Defense
item.description: The next Army chief needs to show that land power is still vital and relevant for modern warfare that’s heavily focused on drones, writes John G. Ferrari in this op-ed.

item.title: 🏴 Europe defense autonomy is in reach at €50 billion a year: German experts | Defense News
item.description: A new paper identifies “ten central capability gaps” Europe needs to plug to be able to act autonomously, without U.S. military assistance.

item.title: 📍 A small town in Germany braces for end to decades of life with US troops | Defense News
item.description: A picturesque Bavarian town has hosted U.S. forces since the end of WWII and could bear the brunt of the U.S. military's withdrawal from Germany.

item.title: 🌍 The future of US energy security: Building on lessons from the Iran war | Atlantic Council
item.description: The global energy disruption caused by the Iran war reveals what US energy policy has gotten right—and wrong. These lessons could pave the way toward a more energy-secure world. The post The future of US energy security: Building on lessons from the Iran war appeared first on…

item.title: 📍 To realize returns on their AI investments, corporations must consider their workers | Atlantic Council
item.description: For business leaders to benefit from the AI transformation, they need to prioritize workers and earn their trust. The post To realize returns on their AI investments, corporations must consider their workers appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon turns to AI targeting to help troops shoot drones | Defense News
item.description: The technology can detect threats and distinguish them from non-threats, such as birds, faster than a human operator can.
